Description: Test with standard, well-formatted company names
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: 'Microsoft Corporation'
• Test: 'Apple Inc.'
• Test: 'Tesla, Inc.'

Description: Test with null, empty, or whitespace-only inputs
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: '' (empty string)
• Test: '   ' (whitespace)
• Test: NULL value

Description: Test with special characters and symbols
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: '@#$%^&*()'
• Test: 'Company™ & Co.®'
• Test: 'Café Résumé'

Description: Test with misspelled or incomplete company names
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: 'Microsft' (misspelled)
• Test: 'App' (incomplete)
• Test: 'Goog' (abbreviated)

Description: Test with generic names matching multiple entities
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: 'Delta' (airline/faucets/dental)
• Test: 'Mercury' (payments/insurance/planet)
• Test: 'Amazon' (company/river)

Description: Test different case variations of company names
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: 'microsoft'
• Test: 'MICROSOFT'
• Test: 'MiCrOsOfT'

Description: All mandatory and optional fields populated
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test with Fortune 500 companies
• Verify all 150+ fields have values
• Check data richness score

Description: Only some fields populated for lesser-known entities
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test with small startups
• Test with private companies
• Verify graceful degradation

Description: No data generated for non-existent companies
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: 'NonExistentCompanyXYZ123'
• Test: 'FakeCorpABC'
• Verify appropriate null handling

Description: Critical fields present, optional fields missing
Example Test Scenarios:
• Verify: Company Name, Category always present
• Allow: Awards, ESG Practices to be empty
• Test classification of mandatory vs optional

Description: Related fields populated together or empty together
Example Test Scenarios:
• If CEO Name exists, CEO LinkedIn should exist
• If funding exists, investors should exist
• Check logical field groupings

Description: Verify against known ground truth data
Example Test Scenarios:
• Cross-check CEO name with LinkedIn
• Verify revenue with SEC filings
• Compare employee count with Crunchbase

• Verify current CEO (not former)
• Check latest funding round date
• Validate recent acquisitions

• Revenue: $50.3B vs $50B vs $50,300M
• Employee count: 10,000 vs 10K vs ~10,000
• Ratings: 4.2 vs 4.20 vs 4

• Year of Incorporation ? CEO tenure start
• Total funding = sum of all rounds
• CAC:LTV ratio = LTV/CAC

Description: Ability to trace data back to sources
Example Test Scenarios:
• Check if sources are cited
• Verify source dates
• Validate source credibility

Description: Detection of invented people, events, or data
Example Test Scenarios:
• Cross-check executive names don't exist
• Verify funding rounds aren't fabricated
• Validate award names are real

• CEO name sounds real but is wrong person
• Funding amount plausible but incorrect
• Office locations exist but company not there

Description: Wrong data presented with high certainty
Example Test Scenarios:
• Assertive statements about unverifiable facts
• Specific numbers that can't be confirmed
• Definitive claims without sources

Description: Derived fields match their source values
Example Test Scenarios:
• CAC:LTV = LTV ÷ CAC
• YoY Growth = (Rev_Y2 - Rev_Y1)/Rev_Y1
• Burn Multiplier = Net Burn ÷ Net New ARR

Description: Fields logically align with each other
Example Test Scenarios:
• If Profitable, Profits > 0
• If Pre-revenue, Revenue = 0
• If Public, Funding Rounds complete

• Incorporation < First Funding < IPO
• CEO tenure start > Company founding
• Recent news within specified timeframe

• Date format: YYYY-MM-DD or MM/DD/YYYY
• Currency: all USD or mixed with notation
• Phone: +1-XXX-XXX-XXXX vs (XXX) XXX-XXXX

• High hiring velocity + low turnover = growth
• Large employee size + many offices = scale
• High NPS + low churn = customer satisfaction

• Test: Companies < 6 months old
• Test: Stealth mode startups
• Test: Recently announced companies

• Test: Berkshire Hathaway (conglomerate)
• Test: Alphabet (holding company)
• Test: Multinational corporations

• Test: Private equity owned
• Test: Family-owned businesses
• Test: Unlisted companies

Description: Very large numbers at upper bounds
Example Test Scenarios:
• Employee count: 2,000,000+ (Walmart)
• Revenue: $500B+ (Amazon)
• Market cap: $3T+ (Apple)

Description: Legitimate zero values in numeric fields
Example Test Scenarios:
• Zero offices beyond HQ (remote company)
• Zero funding (bootstrapped)
• Zero turnover (very new/stable)

• Negative profits (loss-making)
• Negative growth rate (declining)
• Negative cash flow

• Market share: 0-100%
• Churn rate: 0-100%
• YoY growth: can exceed 100%
• Profit margin: can be negative

• Future dates (invalid for historical)
• Very old dates (1800s)
• Year 2000 (Y2K edge cases)

Description: Ratios with special meaning at extremes
Example Test Scenarios:
• CAC:LTV = 0 (no CAC or infinite LTV)
• CAC:LTV < 1 (unprofitable acquisition)
• Burn multiplier = ? (no new ARR)

Description: Correct data types for each field
Example Test Scenarios:
• Numbers stored as numbers, not strings
• Dates in date format
• Booleans as true/false

• Test: Valid URLs (200 status)
• Test: Broken links (404)
• Test: Redirects (301/302)

• Countries: comma-separated
• Office locations: consistent structure
• Investor list: standardized format

• Short name: < 50 chars
• Overview: 200-500 chars
• Description fields: < 2000 chars

• Test multiple 'Delta' companies in batch
• Test 'Apple Inc.' then 'Apple Bank'
• Test name variations consecutively

Description: Events after LLM training data cutoff
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: Recent CEO changes (2025)
• Test: Latest funding rounds
• Test: Current product launches

• Recent acquisitions not in training data
• New subsidiaries
• Corporate spin-offs

• New market entrants
• Changed market leaders
• Disrupted industries

• New GDPR enforcement
• Updated industry regulations
• Changed certification requirements

• Recent layoffs
• Public controversies
• Business disruptions

• Test: 'Delta' (multiple industries)
• Test: 'Target' (retail vs. brand agency)
• Verify correct entity selected

• Test: Google vs Alphabet
• Test: Instagram vs Meta
• Test: YouTube vs Google

• Test: Unilever (UK vs Netherlands)
• Test: Royal Dutch Shell
• Test: Multinational with separate entities

• Test: IBM vs International Business Machines
• Test: AT&T vs American Telephone & Telegraph
• Test: 3M vs Minnesota Mining and Manufacturing

• Test: Meta Platforms, Inc. vs Meta
• Test: X Corp vs Twitter
• Verify correct legal entity identification

• Test: Seed stage startup ? 'Startup'
• Test: 500-employee company ? 'SMB'
• Test: Sequoia Capital ? 'VC'

• Test: Stripe ? Financial Technology
• Test: Tesla ? Automotive & Clean Energy
• Test: Amazon ? E-commerce & Cloud

• Test: SpaceX ? Private
• Test: Apple ? Public
• Test: WhatsApp ? Subsidiary

• Test: Brand sentiment (positive/neutral/negative)
• Test: Glassdoor rating categorization
• Test: NPS score interpretation

• Test: Customer concentration risk
• Test: Geopolitical risk level
• Test: Burn rate risk assessment

Description: Generation time for different company types
Example Test Scenarios:
• Measure: Time for Fortune 500 company
• Measure: Time for startup
• Compare: Public vs private company time

• Test: Companies with long descriptions
• Test: Many office locations
• Verify: No mid-sentence cutoffs

• Test: Company A then Company B
• Verify: No data from A in B's profile
• Test: Batch of similar companies

Description: Graceful handling when data doesn't exist
Example Test Scenarios:
• Test: Private company financials
• Test: Undisclosed funding amounts
• Verify: Appropriate null/NA/unknown values

Description: Fields that don't apply to entity type
Example Test Scenarios:
• VC firm: 'Products' = N/A
• Bootstrapped: 'Investors' = N/A
• Remote company: 'Office Locations' = N/A

Description: Unclear if data exists or just not found
Example Test Scenarios:
• Data might exist but not public
• Data not yet generated (new company)
• Data previously existed but removed

• Don't default: Revenue = $0
• Can default: Remote policy = 'Unknown'
• Context-dependent defaults

• If Revenue = null, YoY Growth = null
• If CAC = null, CAC:LTV = null
• Appropriate null arithmetic

• Flag: Estimated vs confirmed values
• Indicate: Public vs inferred data
• Score: Data confidence level

Description: Primary vs secondary vs tertiary sources
Example Test Scenarios:
• Tier 1: Official company website, SEC filings
• Tier 2: Crunchbase, LinkedIn, press releases
• Tier 3: News articles, blog posts

• Recent: Last 6 months
• Acceptable: Last 12 months
• Outdated: >12 months

• Combine: Accuracy + Completeness + Recency
• Weight by field importance
• Provide quality grade (A-F)
